HRREC-92016
PH: Candidate attaches non-supported files

29% — limited tension in this heuristic pass; lenses largely agree
  • Jira — bounce/complaint telemetry — confirm surfaced strings vs silent drop.; attachments — align client vs server size/type rejection.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If internal threads mention attachment limits, align on-screen hints with what the system rejects for this slice.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA which admin toggles mirror common customer misconfiguration for this area.
  • Jira — Test attachment boundary errors this story owns.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, ask Salomon whether bounce copy is visible—not silent.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) Same catalog snapshot as the row above (one epic-wide search in this replay). For HRREC-92016, align attachment variable containers and send REST hooks with the size/type rules in Jira. Risk — Same catalog-level risk as rows above—confirm wiring for this key.
  • Finding: No strong cross-lens conflict showed up in this heuristic replay.
  • Recommended next step: Re-read Jira for “UI displays a system message for blocke...” on HRREC-92016; if nothing new surfaces, keep scenarios as written.

Beyond Jira — UI displays a system message for blocked/unsupported file types
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: a candidate replies with an attachment using a...
When A hard bounce or complaint path fires for a recruiting email in this slice; I open the thread or reporting surfaces Jira names
Then I see the specific failure headline/body or task status Jira promises — not a silent send without surfaced handling

Delivery failure surfaces
Given the integration produces a hard bounce or complaint path the story references
When I review the thread and any notification or reporting surfaces in scope
Then I see the specific status or copy promised (not a silent drop without analytics or suppression alignment)

Attachment limits client and server
Given I stage allowed and disallowed files and sizes around the published limit
When I attempt send
Then client validation, server rejection, and recruiter-visible error strings stay aligned (no orphan partial sends)
